• Memory
  • DMA

1 Memory Subsystem

The AM62x memory subsystem integrates SRAM, ECC, and cache controllers for high-reliability data storage. All memory regions support ECC protection.

1.1 ECC Configuration

Error Correction Code (ECC) provides SECDED protection for all SRAM regions. ECC is enabled via the ECC_CTRL register at base address 0x40000000.

To enable ECC: set bit 0 (ECC_EN) of ECC_CTRL. On detection of a correctable error the ECC_STATUS register bit 0 is set and an interrupt is raised if ECC_IRQ_EN is configured. The faulting address is latched in ECC_ERR_ADDR.

1.1.1 ECC Register Map

ECC_CTRL (0x40000000): ECC enable control register. Bit 0 = ECC_EN. ECC_STATUS (0x40000004): Error status. Bit 0 = correctable error. ECC_ERR_ADDR (0x40000008): Address of faulting memory access. ECC_IRQ_EN (0x4000000C): Interrupt enable register.

1.2 SRAM Controller

The SRAM controller manages access arbitration between CPU, DMA, and peripheral masters. Four banks of 256 KB are available with independent power gating. The sram_init() function implements the initialization sequence.

2 DMA Controller

The DMA controller provides eight independent channels for memory-to-memory, memory-to-peripheral, and peripheral-to-memory transfers.

2.1 DMA Channel Configuration

Each channel is configured via a descriptor: source address, destination address, transfer count, and control word. Start a transfer by writing the descriptor to DMA_DESC[ch] and setting DMA_CTRL[ch].EN.

2.2 DMA Transfer Modes

Single-shot, circular, and chain (scatter-gather) modes are supported. All modes support 32-bit, 16-bit, and 8-bit element widths. Misaligned transfers raise a bus error in DMA_ERR_STATUS.

AM62x Technical Reference Manual Rev 4.2
